title: Caramelized Crunchy Brazilian Banana Stir-fry
description: A salty and chewy one-pot Brazilian dish featuring fried ripe bananas, caramelized to perfection in a single pot.
instructions: Preheat a skillet over medium heat. Halve and slice the bananas into thin rounds.
In a small bowl, mix baking soda with 1 tablespoon of water to make an alkaline solution for neutralizing the natural starches in the banana skins.
Coat half of the banana slices lightly with butter on both sides and place them in the skillet. Brush them gently with the baking soda solution.
Cook until golden brown, about 2 minutes per side. Flip and brush with more baking soda solution if needed to get a nice caramelized exterior.
Once all banana rounds are fried, remove from heat. In the same pan, melt remaining butter over medium-low heat.

Stir in brown sugar until completely melted and dissolved. Add the cooked banana slices back into the skillet, toss well with the caramel sauce, and cook for an additional 1 minute or until fully coated.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate. Garnish with extra salt if desired.
